alternatives_rejected: Rate the legacy in-process throttle delay (P2-watch) as a P2 -- rejected: doc 10 Slack Dedupe And Suppression forbids SUPPRESSING (dropping/coalescing-away) a first stop/escalate; the legacy _digest_packet_to_send only DELAYS such a digest by at most one min_digest_interval_seconds poll and re-emits it via pending_due, after which decide()'s never-suppress arm fires -- the digest still SENDS, so it is not a suppression; recorded as the non-blocking P3-10d-2-1 instead.

Rate the _packet_recommended_route heuristic (P2-route) as a P2 -- rejected: the derived route feeds only the dedupe KEY; a wrong derivation can only make the key MORE distinct (a spurious new first-seen key -> a spurious SEND), it cannot collapse two genuinely-different states onto one SENT key in a way that suppresses a real escalation, and the never-suppress arms key off whether THIS key is first-seen -- so a wrong route causes only a spurious send, never a missed escalation (the conservative direction); the Slice-10e step-4 evidence_mode wiring will give the packet a cleaner typed control_plane fact.

Treat the updated test test_supervisor_digest_compose_failure_marks_pending_claim_failed as a weakening -- rejected: every original assertion is kept and one is added; the pre-10d-2 test ran with NO dedupe store so a non-escalation background digest reached the compose path directly, whereas post-10d-2 the doc-10-correct behavior is fail-quiet, so the test was updated to wire a working dedupe store (the realistic production config) where a first-seen digest decides should_send=True and the compose path is still reached -- the genuine behavior change is correctly tested, no safety/boundary/reason-code coverage dropped.

decision: Slice 11 begins with sub-slice 11a: the lowest-risk extraction (pure data types) that proves the import-shim pattern without any behavior risk. The pattern established by 11a is the template for every later 11.x extraction: each PR is refactor-only unless explicitly the final atomic-landing step (11.13 in doc 11; absent here per the construction-vs-rollout distinction), each PR carries a four-question answer + a shim audit + a minimum proof bundle, and each PR's extracted module never imports back into implementation.py.
alternatives_rejected: Start Slice 11 with git_service.py instead of types.py -- REJECTED because doc 11 Refactoring Steps explicitly order types first so later extractions can preserve monkeypatch + import compatibility; types are the dependency leaves that everything else imports.

Move DagArtifactClosureScan and _dedupe_preserving_order together in 11a -- REJECTED because _dedupe_preserving_order has many non-type call sites in implementation.py and moving it broadens the sub-slice scope into non-type code; the prompt's hard rule 'do not move classes that have method bodies depending on outside helpers' applies; defer DagArtifactClosureScan to a later sub-slice that owns the helper.

Move all the private routing constants (_COMMIT_HYGIENE_ROUTE etc.) to types.py too -- REJECTED because they are not part of a TYPE contract; they are routing-helper constants. Only the one default-value constant for DagAuthorityGateOutcome.route would qualify, and we inline that as a literal to avoid duplicating a constant whose primary uses are in routing helpers we are not moving.

Land Slice 11 as one big atomic refactor PR -- REJECTED because doc 11 explicitly orders the extractions and requires per-PR proof bundles + targeted-test gates; staging via small extraction PRs keeps each PR independently reversible and bounded-risk.

After the pre-sort, _dedup_refs_keep_first picks the canonically-smallest sibling at each collision deterministically; the set-level idempotency_key is invariant under input reordering by construction. Document the canonical-sort discipline + the intentional authority-omission from the per-ref digest payload in the module docstring (P3-A1). Replace the misleading test (P3-A5-mislead) with a test that asserts the new canonical-keep-first semantics. Add 2 new tests pinning the P1-13e-1 invariant: a minimal collision reproducer + a real-corpus permutation subsample. NO behavior change to other governance modules (models.py / ingestor.py / journal_parser.py / decision_log_parser.py FROZEN per the implementer's chunk-shape point 8; the finalizer scopes edits to evidence_set.py + the 2 affected test files only). Carry P3-13e-4 NEW: doc-13:221-222 vs prompt mismatch on duplicate compatibility-projection dedup policy -- resolve in a future sub-slice possibly via per-authority dedup policy.
alternatives_rejected: Option B (sort within each collision group during dedup) -- equivalent invariant but structurally less clean than Option A (Option A pre-sorts the full list once; Option B would require collecting all siblings at each (authority, ref_id) key then sorting per-group). Option A is the reviewer's preferred path.

Drop the sort-invariance claim and accept input-order-sensitive idempotency_key -- rejected per the orchestrator prompt's chunk-shape point 2 which mandates set-level idempotency; an input-order-sensitive key fails this contract.

Include authority in the per-ref digest payload -- rejected per the reviewer's P3-A1 guidance: doc-13:186 wording names the digest contract over source-id + content-digest only; authority is part of the (authority, ref_id) tuple that governs dedup-identity but NOT the per-ref content digest. The set-level digest remains distinguishing via the canonical sort key which includes authority.

Delete the misleading test instead of replacing -- rejected because the test name + body encoded a behavior pin; replacing with a test that asserts the new canonical-keep-first semantics preserves the test coverage and explicitly documents the P1-13e-1 fix in the test surface.

Edit governance/journal_parser.py / decision_log_parser.py to dedup at parser level instead of digester level -- rejected because (a) those modules are FROZEN for 13e per the implementer's chunk-shape point 8; (b) the digester is the natural composition step where multiple input lanes converge so per-ref dedup belongs there; (c) the bug is in the digester's keep-first ordering, not in the parsers' anchor emission.

Resolve the doc-13:221-222 vs prompt mismatch in this finalizer -- rejected because the implementer correctly followed the prompt; the doc/prompt discrepancy is a separate decision requiring product-owner input (which authority gets keep-ALL vs keep-first). Carry as P3-13e-4 for a future sub-slice.

decision: PostgresGovernanceEvidenceStore is async (mirrors regroup_overlay_store.py async pattern); the 13g ABC declares sync signatures but ABCMeta only checks method presence/name, and asyncpg cannot be invoked synchronously. The in-memory store stays sync (no IO). The two concretes satisfy the same ABC contract semantically but have different async/sync signatures — the parametrized contract test runs both via an async wrapper so they appear equivalent at the test layer. asyncpg connection injected via constructor (matches RegroupOverlayStore); fail-closed on corpus_id collision via in-transaction SELECT check + raise (mirrors regroup_overlay_store.py:615-621 fail-closed digest check pattern); canonical JSONB via json.dumps(..., sort_keys=True, separators=(",", ":")) mirroring _jsonb at regroup_overlay_store.py:305-306; typed-input-validation guard via isinstance(GovernanceEvidenceSet) check mirroring 13g store.py:313-326 (P3-13g-R2 fix). Schema mirrors the evidence_nodes precedent at schema.sql:457-519 (typed-row + JSONB + CHECK constraint + UNIQUE).
alternatives_rejected: Synchronous Postgres store — rejected because asyncpg is async-only and the rest of the execution_control/* stores are async (MergeQueueStore / RegroupOverlayStore). A sync store would either require psycopg (introducing a new dependency) or block the event loop on asyncio.run() per call (anti-pattern).

Add a separate GovernanceEvidenceStoreAsync ABC — rejected because the 13g ABC is the doc-13:188-190 contract; minting a parallel ABC would fragment the contract surface. The async/sync divergence is acceptable per the ABCMeta semantics (the ABC enforces method presence, not signature parity).

Use raw SQL strings inline (no _jsonb helper) — rejected because the regroup_overlay_store precedent at line 305-306 establishes _jsonb as the canonical JSONB serializer; reusing it preserves byte-identity with sibling stores.

Skip the parametrized contract test — rejected because the chunk-shape STATUS.md point 6 explicitly mandates the parametrized test confirming the two concretes are functionally interchangeable (one ABC; two concretes; one set of contract assertions).
Add the new readers as top-level package exports — N/A (this sub-slice has no new readers; the Postgres store IS the new export at __all__ position 22).

decision: All 5 mechanical type-narrowing edits applied as planned. The typing_extensions.TypedDict import was the only deviation from the BEFORE entry plan -- Pydantic v2 requires it on Python <3.12 per the documented Pydantic error link. The __pydantic_config__ = ConfigDict(extra='allow') on both TypedDicts preserves the doc-13:113-126 + doc-13:128-141 dict-permissive runtime behavior: producers can populate ANY key (not just the 5/12 documented ones), and Pydantic admits the dict without complaint. The TypedDicts serve as static-analysis + documentation tools naming the documented producer-side keys, NOT as runtime gates. This preserves backward compatibility with the 13a-13l producer + test surfaces (which exercise free-form keys like window_start / window_end / cursor / z_key / a_key / file_mtime / mtime / commit / row_version / freshness). alternatives_rejected: Use typing.TypedDict instead of typing_extensions.TypedDict: rejected -- Pydantic v2 fails closed at schema generation time with PydanticUserError per https://errors.pydantic.dev/2.12/u/typed-dict-version (the project targets Python 3.11+ and Pydantic v2 requires typing_extensions on <3.12 so per-key annotations land in the runtime schema). The typing_extensions backport is the documented Pydantic-supported approach.

Use TypedDict without __pydantic_config__ extra='allow': rejected -- Pydantic v2 defaults to extra='forbid' for TypedDict shapes (mirroring the BaseModel extra='forbid' precedent at workflows/develop/execution/verification.py:74 + failure_router.py:576); this would BREAK the 13a-13l test fixtures (test_evidence_set_required_field_set_and_defaults uses source_window={'start': 1, 'end': 2}; test_page_ref_round_trip_with_optional_ranges uses stale_check={'commit': 'abc123', 'row_version': 7}; etc. -- many fixtures populate free-form keys). Reproduced: `pytest tests/test_execution_control_prompt_context_adapter.py tests/workflows/develop/execution/test_dispatcher.py -q` => 3 failed, 42 passed. Fix approach (reviewer-recommended option 3): drop importlib.reload entirely and rely on the already-imported PromptContextBundle.model_fields + PromptContextBundle.model_config inspection that the test already performs at lines 544-562. Rationale: (a) the structural invariant (10 legacy fields + extra='allow' base config) is fully observable on the already-imported class -- no reload needed; (b) reload of the dispatcher module replaces module-level globals (Lock singletons, FakeStore/FakeRuntime test classes pulled from dispatcher.py) with fresh instances, breaking isinstance checks + shared-state invariants in subsequently-run dispatcher tests within the same pytest session; (c) consistent with the auto-memory feedback_no_silent_degradation + feedback_harden_all_code_paths rules (no silent corruption of cross-suite test state). The unused `import importlib` line at adapter test file line 61 will also be removed (it is the only reload-related dependency).

p3_self_noted: P3-14-2-1 (implementer-self-noted; cosmetic; non-blocking) -- make_stdlib_subprocess_runner factory uses synthetic '--' sentinel in the args list to delimit the canonical-JSON stdin payload from the Git argv proper. The fake-runner pattern preserves '--' + payload in the invocation trace so tests can inspect what content the writer wrote; the production factory then splits on '--' and routes the payload via subprocess.run(input=stdin_bytes). The asymmetric convention is intentional (testing without shell-out per the implementer prompt MUST NOT shell out to real git in unit tests) but a future Slice 14 maintenance pass could replace the sentinel with a typed GitSubprocessInvocation(args, stdin) shape if multiple writers grow up that need stdin routing.

p3_self_noted: P3-14-3-1 (implementer-self-noted; cosmetic; non-blocking) -- reader _make_gap_finding populates CommitProvenanceGapFinding.commit_hash + precommit_provenance_ref with a STRUCTURAL CORRELATOR built from the query target (line_provenance_read:{repo_id}:{ref}:{path}:{line_start}-{line_end}) rather than with the result-side commit hash + canonical ref the writer gap finding uses. Semantic divergence intentional (read-side gaps correlate to QUERIES, writer-side to COMMITS) but a future Slice 14 maintenance pass could split CommitProvenanceGapFinding into CommitProvenanceWriteGapFinding + CommitProvenanceReadGapFinding to avoid field overloading. Cosmetic carry.

P3-14-3-2 (implementer-self-noted; cosmetic; non-blocking) -- LineProvenanceReadResult.is_eligible_for_downstream_consumers is a Python @property not a Pydantic field. Keeps typed-surface contract minimal (eligibility derives from completeness so a field would be redundant + invite drift) but means callers cannot rely on the flag in model_dump_json output. If a downstream JSON consumer requires the explicit flag, future Slice 14 sub-slices could add a @computed_field annotation.

P3-14-3-3 (implementer-self-noted; cosmetic; non-blocking) -- reader _resolve_one_commit consults PayloadStore.get_payload_from_notes BEFORE get_payload_by_ref. Order asymmetric vs writer; reader prefers notes (keyed by commit hash; do not require precommit_provenance_digest derivation). Future sub-slices may consult both AND verify cross-consistency.

p3_self_noted: P3-14-4-1 (implementer-self-noted; cosmetic; non-blocking) -- LineageEmitterInputs carries 18 fields, several of which are optional Git metadata (new_author_timestamp + new_committer_timestamp + new_parent_count + original_author_timestamp + original_committer_timestamp + original_parent_count) that are only used by specific detection branches (amend uses committer timestamps; squash uses parent count; recovery uses new_committer_timestamp). The optional-with-default pattern keeps the typed surface flat (per feedback_flat_structured_output) but means callers can construct inputs that under-specify a detection scenario (e.g. omit committer timestamps and the amend detection silently does not fire). Future sub-slice may add a typed shape per detection scenario (RebaseDetectionInputs, AmendDetectionInputs, etc.) at the cost of multiplying the typed surface area; current single-shape pattern mirrors the 2nd sub-slice CommitProvenanceWriterInputs.

P3-14-4-2 (implementer-self-noted; cosmetic; non-blocking) -- detect_rewrite_candidates uses a fixed-priority CASCADE: it returns ALL candidates that fire (the emitter then decides ambiguity), but the emit_for_repo logic treats any multi-DISTINCT-reason result as ambiguous regardless of priority. An alternative would let callers configure detection-priority weights (e.g. cherry-pick > amend when both fire) to resolve ambiguity automatically. The current 'reject ambiguous unless lineage is recorded' interpretation matches doc-14:208-209 verbatim, but the priority-weighted variant may be useful for downstream wiring; mark for future Slice 14 maintenance pass. Cosmetic.

P3-14-4-3 (implementer-self-noted; cosmetic; non-blocking) -- InMemoryLineageWalker has NO cross-process persistence: the lineage records are persisted to refs/iriai/lineage/{digest} + Git notes, but the in-memory walker is INSTANCE-SCOPED. A re-loaded process must rebuild the walker by scanning the lineage namespace; this sub-slice does NOT provide a rebuild-on-startup helper. The omission is intentional (this sub-slice's scope is the emitter + multi-repo audit per doc-14:174-178; the rebuild-on-startup wiring is naturally Slice 14's last sub-slice or the production-wiring task; the in-memory walker is testable + production-suitable for a single-process governance projection). Cosmetic.

Scope is a single NEW test file tests/test_execution_control_governance_activation_boundary.py (~600-900 lines; >=30 tests; target ~40-60) that asserts at module-source-scan time that NO governance module (22 in total: 6 Slice 17 sub-slice modules + 16 prior Slice 13/13A/14/15/16 modules) imports a consumer-side activation/mutation module (failure_router, dashboard, supervisor, merge_queue, regroup_overlay, scheduler_metrics, scheduler_sizing, git_service, repair, verification, gates, post_dag_gates, post_test_guard, runtime_client, sandbox, workspace_authority); the Slice 13A typed-adapter pattern (prompt_context_adapter.py + dispatcher_prompt_context.py) is ALLOWED-by-design per doc-13a:42-46 + 124-126 since those imports are typed-shape READS not activation mutators.
